front: Opera: love triangle between an Ethiopian princess enslaved in Egypt, the Egyptian military commander Radames, and the Egyptian princess Amneris
Opera: premiere was delayed because scenery and costumes were trapped in Paris during the Siege of Paris
Opera: Roberto Alagna walked off the La Scala stage after being booed; replaced mid-performance by Antonello Palombi in street clothes
Opera: Leontyne Price became the first African American to sing a leading role at La Scala in this role (1960); her 1985 Met farewell was also in this role
Opera: Angel Blue withdrew from a 2022 Verona Arena production to protest Anna Netrebko's use of blackface makeup for the title role
Opera: Radames is entombed alive for treason; the title character chooses to die with him, singing the duet 'La fatal pietra'
Opera: title character's opening leitmotif is 'short A, A-sharp, B, C-sharp, B'
Opera: third act opens with first violins playing quiet staccato sixteenth-note Gs sweeping up and down through four octaves
Opera: chorus shouts 'Guerra, guerra, guerra!' in Act II
Opera: Amneris sings 'L'aborrita rivale a me sfuggia' before the Judgement Scene where Radames refuses to speak in his defense
Opera: Maria Callas interpolated a high E-flat at the end of Act II finale 'Gloria all'Egitto' during Mexico City performances

Opera: soprano aria in which Aida laments she will never see her Ethiopian homeland again; climaxes on sustained pianissimo high C, then falling sixteenth-note thirds into 'non ti vedrò, non ti vedrò mai più'
Opera: soprano aria in which Aida is torn between wishing her lover to triumph and loyalty to her father and Ethiopian homeland; ends with 'Numi, pietà'
Opera: Act II Triumphal Scene features a solo trumpet melody beginning short E-flat, long A-flat, triplet B-flat, E-flat, B-flat
back: Grand March / Triumphal Scene (Act II) from Aida (Verdi)

front: Opera: during the Triumphal Scene, Amneris tricks the title character into admitting her love for Radames
back: Grand March / Triumphal Scene (Act II) from Aida (Verdi)
